meta_title: Impact Windows in Pembroke Park, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Pembroke Park, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 60+ yrs experience, financing from $87/mo. Get a free quote today!
h1: Impact Windows in Pembroke Park, FL — Built for the Storms That Have Shaped This Community
intro:
Pembroke Park may sit just five miles from the Atlantic coastline, but don't let that distance fool you. When a major hurricane rolls through Broward County, communities like Pembroke Park feel the full force of sustained winds, flying debris, and the kind of structural stress that older homes were simply never designed to handle. For the homeowners of Pembroke Park Estates and the surrounding residential streets that wind through ZIP codes 33009 and 33023, upgrading to impact-resistant windows isn't just a smart investment — it's a necessary step toward protecting your family, your property, and your financial future.

With an average home value of approximately $270,000, most Pembroke Park properties represent the single largest asset a family owns. And with roughly 55% of homes in the area built before 1994 — meaning they predate the sweeping reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew — a significant portion of the housing stock was constructed to standards that modern engineers would consider inadequate for South Florida's storm environment. Single-pane glass, aluminum frames with no thermal break, and standard exterior doors with basic hardware are common findings in homes throughout this community. These are vulnerabilities that impact windows directly address, replacing outdated materials with laminated glass and multi-chamber frames engineered to withstand 170 mph wind loads as required under Broward County's wind zone designation.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped homeowners throughout the greater Hollywood, Hallandale Beach, Miramar, and West Park corridor — including countless families right here in Pembroke Park — make the transition to impact windows with confidence. Our team brings more than 60 years of combined installation experience to every project, and we perform 100% in-house work with no subcontractors. Whether you're near the C.B. Smith Park area or tucked into a quiet cul-de-sac in Pembroke Park Estates, we understand the specific demands of this neighborhood and what it takes to bring your home up to today's code standards and beyond.

section_why.h2: Why Pembroke Park Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact window company is about more than picking a product off a catalog. It's about finding a team that understands your neighborhood's building stock, your county's code requirements, and the real-world storm threats your home will face over the next 20 to 30 years. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by delivering exactly that — personalized, expert-level installation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, with a track record that includes more than 500 completed homes and an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau.

What sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart in Pembroke Park specifically is our deep familiarity with the local housing landscape. We know that a large share of homes here were built in the 1970s and 1980s, often with non-standard window openings, aging rough bucks, and original construction methods that require careful inspection before any new window can be properly installed. Our in-house installation crews — not outsourced laborers — conduct thorough site assessments before a single frame is ordered, ensuring the products we specify will fit correctly, seal properly, and meet the Town of Pembroke Park Building Department's permitting requirements without delays or costly corrections.

We carry two of the industry's most respected impact window lines: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are tested and certified to meet Florida Product Approval standards, and our team is factory-trained on both product families. This means we're not just installers — we're product specialists who can match the right frame style, glass package, and hardware configuration to your specific home and budget. From single-hung impact windows for a modest three-bedroom home to large picture windows that frame a beautifully landscaped backyard, we treat every opening with the same level of care and precision.

Finally, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it realistic for lower-middle income households in Pembroke Park to access the same level of storm protection that higher-priced neighborhoods take for granted. We believe every South Florida family deserves a safe, energy-efficient home, and our flexible payment plans are designed to make that possible without financial strain.

section_history.h2: Pembroke Park's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Windows
section_history.body:
Pembroke Park has been in the path of nine named storms since 1990, and three of those events left lasting marks on the community. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 remains the defining storm in South Florida's modern memory — a Category 5 behemoth that obliterated entire neighborhoods to the south and sent a clear message that standard residential construction was dangerously inadequate. While Andrew's worst damage was concentrated in Homestead and South Miami-Dade, Broward County communities like Pembroke Park experienced wind gusts well above 100 mph and widespread structural damage that took years to fully repair. Andrew was ultimately the catalyst for a complete overhaul of the Florida Building Code, but homes built before that transformation remain standing throughout this community.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 hit Broward County particularly hard, tracking across the peninsula in a matter of hours but generating sustained winds that caused tremendous damage to roofs, windows, and exterior doors throughout the region. For many Pembroke Park homeowners, Wilma was the storm that broke windows they thought were durable, pushed storm shutters off their tracks, and left interiors exposed to hours of wind-driven rain. And in 2017, Hurricane Irma served as a sobering reminder that South Florida's storm exposure is an ongoing reality — not a historical footnote. Irma's outer bands battered Broward County with tropical-storm-to-hurricane-force winds, again exposing the weaknesses of homes that hadn't been updated since the pre-Andrew era.

The pattern across all three storms is consistent: homes with impact-resistant windows and doors sustained dramatically less damage than those relying on standard glazing or retrofit storm panels. Impact windows don't just keep glass intact — they maintain the structural envelope of the home, preventing the pressure buildup that leads to roof failures and catastrophic interior flooding. For Pembroke Park families who have lived through at least one of these events, the case for upgrading has never been stronger.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Pembroke Park
section_code.body:
Pembroke Park falls within Broward County's wind zone, which mandates that all new and replacement windows and doors meet hurricane-rated performance standards. The community is not designated as part of the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — that classification applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward's coastal municipalities — but this distinction doesn't reduce the code requirements for impact-rated products. Under the Florida Building Code (FBC), Seventh Edition and beyond, homes in Pembroke Park must use windows and glazing systems that meet the Large Missile Impact test requirements, which includes surviving the impact of a 9-pound 2x4 lumber projectile fired at speeds simulating 170 mph wind loads.

Every impact window installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing in Pembroke Park carries a Florida Product Approval (FPA) number issued by the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation. This approval confirms that the product has been independently tested and certified to meet or exceed the FBC's wind and impact resistance standards for this wind zone. When we submit permits to the Town of Pembroke Park Building Department, we provide the complete FPA documentation, signed and sealed engineering drawings where required, and all necessary product data sheets — ensuring a smooth, compliant approval process that protects both the homeowner and the integrity of the installation.

It's also worth noting that unpermitted window replacements are not only a code violation in Pembroke Park — they can void your homeowner's insurance policy and create serious complications if you ever sell your home. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the full permitting process on your behalf, from initial application through final inspection sign-off, so you can be confident that your new impact windows are fully documented and legally compliant with all Town of Pembroke Park and Broward County building regulations.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Engineered for Pembroke Park Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premier lines of impact-resistant windows in Pembroke Park: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers produce products specifically engineered for the South Florida market, where high humidity, intense UV exposure, salt-laden air, and extreme wind events create a uniquely demanding environment for any building product. We've selected these brands because they consistently deliver on the three qualities that matter most to Pembroke Park homeowners: storm protection, energy efficiency, and long-term durability.

ESW impact windows feature heavy-duty aluminum frames with multi-chamber construction that improves both structural rigidity and thermal performance. The laminated glass units — typically consisting of two panes of tempered or annealed glass bonded to an interlayer of polyvinyl butyral (PVB) — are designed to stay intact even when cracked, preventing dangerous glass shards and maintaining the home's weather barrier throughout a storm event. Low-E coatings on ESW glass reduce solar heat gain by up to 40%, which translates directly into lower cooling costs for Pembroke Park homes during the long South Florida summer. For homes in Pembroke Park Estates with larger window openings or custom architectural shapes, ESW offers a range of configurations including single-hung, double-hung, casement, awning, and fixed picture windows.

CWI impact windows bring a complementary set of performance characteristics, with a particular emphasis on tight tolerances and clean sightlines that are well-suited to the mid-century and late-20th-century architectural styles common in Pembroke Park. CWI's product line includes options with reinforced meeting rails, heavy-gauge frame extrusions, and multiple glass thickness options to accommodate different design pressure requirements. Both ESW and CWI products come with manufacturer warranties that cover frame finish, glass seals, and hardware — and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installation workmanship warranty adds an additional layer of protection for every homeowner we serve.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Pembroke Park — And How to Finance Them
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact window installation in Pembroke Park depends on several factors: the number and size of windows being replaced, the product line selected, the complexity of the existing rough openings, and whether any structural repairs or frame remediation is needed. For a typical three-bedroom, two-bath home in Pembroke Park — a common configuration in this area — homeowners can generally expect a full-home impact window project to range from $8,000 to $18,000, depending on scope and product selection. Homes with larger square footage, multiple sliding glass doors, or custom window shapes may fall at the higher end of that range.

For many Pembroke Park families, the upfront cost of impact windows can feel daunting. That's why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options designed specifically for working-class and lower-middle-income households. Our financing programs start at just $87 per month with $0 down and no prepayment penalties, making it possible to begin protecting your home immediately without depleting your savings. We work with multiple lending partners to find terms that fit your budget, and our team will walk you through all available options during your free in-home consultation.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact windows in Pembroke Park are one of the most financially sound home improvements available. Studies consistently show that impact windows recoup 70–85% of their installation cost in added home value — significant on a $270,000 property. Add in estimated energy savings of 15–30% on annual cooling costs, reduced insurance premiums (often $300 to $800 or more per year), and the elimination of the annual cost and hassle of deploying storm shutters or panels, and the financial case becomes even more compelling. Most Pembroke Park homeowners see their full investment pay back within five to eight years.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Pembroke Park Homeowners Who Install Impact Windows
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact windows in Pembroke Park is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has undergone significant turbulence in recent years, with many carriers exiting the state or dramatically increasing rates for properties that lack hurricane mitigation features. For Pembroke Park homeowners — particularly those in pre-1994 homes that lack modern storm protection — the windstorm portion of a homeowner's policy can represent a substantial annual expense. Impact windows directly address the risk factors that drive those premiums upward.

Florida Statute 627.0629 requires insurance carriers to offer premium discounts for homes with verified hurricane mitigation features, including impact-resistant windows and doors. The amount of the discount varies by carrier and by the specific mitigation measures in place, but Broward County homeowners routinely report windstorm premium reductions of 15% to 45% after completing a full impact window upgrade and obtaining a Wind Mitigation Inspection report. Carriers active in the Pembroke Park market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and others — all recognize impact windows as a qualifying mitigation feature under Florida's wind mitigation credit framework.

The process works as follows: after your impact windows are installed and final inspections are passed, you schedule a Wind Mitigation Inspection with a licensed inspector. The inspector documents the opening protection on your home and issues a report that you submit to your insurance carrier. Premium adjustments are typically applied at your next renewal cycle, and in many cases the annual savings are substantial enough to meaningfully offset your monthly financing payment — effectively making your impact window upgrade partially self-funding from day one. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team can guide you through this process and connect you with trusted local inspectors who serve the Pembroke Park area.

faqs.q: Do Pembroke Park homes really need impact windows if they're not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Pembroke Park sits within a 170 mph wind zone under the Florida Building Code, and inland locations don't significantly reduce wind speeds during a major hurricane. In fact, Andrew's and Wilma's most damaging winds extended well inland across Broward County. Distance from the coast reduces storm surge risk but does little to diminish wind and debris hazards — the primary threats that impact windows are designed to address. With 55% of homes here built before 1994, the need for upgraded window protection is very real.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to install impact windows in a typical Pembroke Park home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: A full-home impact window replacement in Pembroke Park typically ranges from $8,000 to $18,000 depending on home size, window count, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down, making impact windows accessible for families across income levels. During your free in-home estimate, our team will walk you through all available financing options and help you find a payment plan that works for your household budget without compromising on product quality.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace windows in Pembroke Park, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for all window replacements in Pembroke Park under Florida Building Code and Town of Pembroke Park Building Department regulations. Installing windows without a permit is a code violation that can affect your insurance coverage and home resale. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — from submitting the application and Florida Product Approval documentation to scheduling and passing the final inspection. You won't have to deal with the paperwork or the building department at all.

faqs.q: How long does an impact window installation project take for a Pembroke Park home?
faqs.a: Most standard impact window projects in Pembroke Park are completed in one to two days for a typical three- or four-bedroom home. The permit approval process through the Town of Pembroke Park Building Department typically takes one to three weeks, depending on current volume. From the date of your signed contract, most homeowners have their new impact windows installed and inspected within three to six weeks. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will give you a realistic timeline during your consultation and keep you informed at every step of the process.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance in Pembroke Park after installing impact windows?
faqs.a: Broward County homeowners with a full complement of impact-rated windows and doors commonly report windstorm premium reductions of 15% to 45% after completing a Wind Mitigation Inspection and submitting the report to their carrier. On an average Pembroke Park home, that can translate to $300 to $800 or more in annual savings. Combined with energy efficiency gains and the elimination of storm shutter costs, the total annual savings often partially or fully offset a monthly financing payment, making impact windows one of the most financially productive upgrades available to South Florida homeowners.
